messages.subject: [R-sig-genetics] morphometric traits in dapc
messages.body_plain:
Hello, 

this post would be better suited on the adegenet forum. 

If you check ?dapc, you'll see that there is a method for data.frames and matrices, which you can use a input. Any numeric / binary data can be analysed by DAPC this way.

Cheers

Thibaut

--
######################################
Dr Thibaut JOMBART
MRC Centre for Outbreak Analysis and Modelling

From: r-sig-genetics-bounces at r-project.org [r-sig-genetics-bounces at r-project.org] on behalf of Magdalena Herdegen [magherdegen at gmail.com]
Sent: 03 September 2013 08:47
To: r-sig-genetics at r-project.org
Subject: [R-sig-genetics] morphometric traits in dapc

Hello,
I have a question reguarding the format of morphometric data I want to
analyse using the find.clusters and dapc functions. From articles I know it
is possible to analyse this kind of date with dapc, but could not find any
instruction about the correct data format.
I would be greateful for suggestions.

MH

        [[alternative HTML version deleted]]
